name: cargo-mcp
description: MCP server for Cargo workspace operations: check, build, test, clippy, fmt, tree, doc, and clean — callable directly from Copilot Chat with structured JSON output.

commands.title: cargo-mcp: Copy bundled server binary path

cargo-mcp.gettingStarted.markdownDescription: [Open Copilot setup chat](command:cargo-mcp.openSetupChat) — adds the `cargo-mcp` instruction block to your repository so Copilot uses MCP tools instead of the terminal in every future session.

cargo-mcp.binaryPath.markdownDescription: Override the path to the `cargo-mcp` binary. Leave blank to use the binary bundled with the extension. Intended for development against a locally-built `cargo-mcp`.

enumDescriptions: Present a checkbox form for suggestions that need human review.
Automatically accept all suggestions without prompting.
cargo-mcp.elicitationMode.markdownDescription: Controls how `cargo-mcp` handles Clippy/check suggestions that need human approval. `always-skip` is the default and safest choice; `prompt` enables the elicitation dialog; `always-accept` applies all suggestions automatically.

cargo-mcp.dev.progressDelayMs.markdownDescription: **Developer only.** Additional delay in milliseconds inserted after each cargo progress notification. Useful for slowing down progress output to observe the elicitation dialog. Has no effect when `0`.

cargo-mcp.retry.onBusy.markdownDescription: Retry idempotent cargo invocations (`check`, `build`, `test`, `clippy`, `fmt`, `doc`, `tree`, `clean`, `metadata`) when they fail with a transient Windows file-locking error (`(os error 32)` *sharing violation*, `(os error 5)` *access denied*, *being used by another process*). These usually clear themselves within a fraction of a second once an antivirus, file indexer, or stray process releases the handle. Disable if you want failures to surface immediately.

cargo-mcp.retry.delayMs.markdownDescription: Delay in milliseconds between retry attempts when `#cargo-mcp.retry.onBusy#` is enabled. Default is `500` (half a second).

cargo-mcp.retry.maxAttempts.markdownDescription: Maximum total attempts (initial try + retries) when `#cargo-mcp.retry.onBusy#` is enabled. Default is `3`.

cargo-mcp.test.timeoutSecs.markdownDescription: Default wall-clock timeout in seconds applied to every `cargo_test` call that does not supply an explicit `timeout_secs`. The budget covers only test **execution** — the clock starts when compilation and linking finish, so a slow build never trips the timeout. When the budget elapses, cargo and all spawned test binaries are terminated. Set to `0` to disable the default timeout and allow test runs to run indefinitely.

cargo-mcp.extraArgs.markdownDescription: Extra command-line arguments passed to the `cargo-mcp` server on startup. Prefer the dedicated `cargo-mcp.elicitationMode` setting over passing `--elicitation-mode` here.

cargo-mcp.unsafe.windowsRestartManager.markdownDescription: **Memory-unsafe feature — disabled by default.** Enable the Windows **Restart Manager** "who has this file open?" diagnostic. When a `cargo` step fails with a transient file-locking error (e.g. an antivirus or indexer briefly holds a file in `target\`), the server can identify and report the offending process. This is the only feature in the production binary that exercises `unsafe` Win32 FFI; it is encapsulated in [`src/rm/`](https://github.com/MikeGrier/cargo-mcp-rs/tree/main/crates/cargo-mcp/src/rm) and uses Microsoft's typed [`windows-sys`](https://crates.io/crates/windows-sys) bindings, but if you'd rather no `unsafe` code run at all, leave this off. No effect on non-Windows hosts.
